requirement: Every customer must be identified using an Officially Valid Document (OVD) at the commencement of an account-based relationship.
policy_excerpt: At onboarding, every customer must submit an Officially Valid Document (OVD) - passport, Aadhaar, voter ID or driving licence. No account is opened without a verified OVD on file.

requirement: Categorise every customer as low, medium or high risk based on a risk assessment.
policy_excerpt: Each customer is classified as low, medium or high risk at onboarding based on occupation, geography and expected transaction profile. Due diligence is applied in proportion to the risk category.

requirement: Apply customer due diligence proportionate to the assessed risk of each customer.
policy_excerpt: Each customer is classified as low, medium or high risk at onboarding based on occupation, geography and expected transaction profile. Due diligence is applied in proportion to the risk category.

requirement: Carry out Customer Due Diligence (CDD) at the commencement of the relationship.
policy_excerpt: CDD is performed for every customer at the start of the relationship and whenever we doubt the accuracy of previously collected information.

requirement: Carry out Customer Due Diligence (CDD) when there are doubts about previously obtained customer data.
policy_excerpt: CDD is performed for every customer at the start of the relationship and whenever we doubt the accuracy of previously collected information.

requirement: Appoint a Principal Officer responsible for KYC compliance and monitoring.
policy_excerpt: The company has appointed a Principal Officer who is responsible for overseeing KYC compliance and for internal monitoring of customer accounts.

requirement: For customers assessed as high risk, apply enhanced due diligence including obtaining the source of funds.
policy_excerpt: For customers who appear to carry higher risk, additional checks may be carried out at the discretion of the compliance team.
note: EDD generic and discretionary; no source-of-funds

requirement: When onboarding via the Video-based Customer Identification Process (V-CIP), record the customer's live location (geo-tagging) and store a recording of the full interaction.
policy_excerpt: Customers may be onboarded remotely through a video call in which an officer verifies the customer's OVD and photograph.
note: video onboarding present but no geo-tagging or recording

requirement: Update the KYC records of high-risk customers at least once every two years.
policy_excerpt: Customer KYC information is reviewed and updated from time to time based on the customer's risk category, so that our records remain current.
note: updation addressed but no two-year cadence

requirement: Carry out Customer Due Diligence (CDD) when a specified transaction threshold is crossed.
policy_excerpt: CDD is performed for every customer at the start of the relationship and whenever we doubt the accuracy of previously collected information.

requirement: For legal-entity customers, identify the beneficial owner(s) and verify their identity.
policy_excerpt: At onboarding, every customer must submit an Officially Valid Document (OVD) - passport, Aadhaar, voter ID or driving licence. No account is opened without a verified OVD on file.

requirement: File Suspicious Transaction Reports (STRs) and Cash Transaction Reports (CTRs) with FIU-IND within the prescribed timelines.
policy_excerpt: The company has appointed a Principal Officer who is responsible for overseeing KYC compliance and for internal monitoring of customer accounts.

requirement: Maintain records of client identity and transactions for at least five years after the business relationship ends or the account is closed.
policy_excerpt: Records of customer identity and transactions are retained for a period of three years after the account is closed.
note: policy mandates 3 years, contradicting the 5-year minimum
